critic_raw:
SCOUTER's slot attention module introduces additional architectural complexity relative to a fully connected baseline. If the hyperparameter tuning budget for SCOUTER (number of slots, attention temperature, slot dimensions) was substantially larger than for the baseline, the 'similar classification accuracy' comparison is not controlled for tuning effort. The baseline may be underfit relative to SCOUTER.

Explanation evaluation metrics for visual explanations (e.g., pointing game accuracy, insertion/deletion AUC) measure proxy properties: whether the highlighted region overlaps with ground-truth objects, or whether masking the highlighted region changes the prediction. Neither metric verifies that the explanation is faithful to the model's actual decision process — a slot attending to an incorrect region for the right reason can pass both tests.

The 'better confusion matrix metrics on binary classification' claim is sensitive to class imbalance. Precision and recall trade off against each other based on classification threshold, and F1 is sensitive to the positive class definition. If the binary split is unbalanced, SCOUTER's confusion matrix improvement may reflect implicit threshold adjustment rather than a structural improvement in classification.
all_issues_raised: If SCOUTER's hyperparameter tuning budget (number of slots, attention temperature, dimensions) substantially exceeds the baseline's, the 'similar classification accuracy' comparison is not controlled for tuning effort.

Standard visual explanation metrics (pointing game, insertion/deletion) measure proxy properties, not faithfulness to the model's actual decision process; a slot attending to an incorrect region for the right reason can pass both.

Confusion matrix metric improvements are threshold-sensitive and class-imbalance-sensitive; the claimed advantage may reflect implicit threshold adjustment rather than structural classification improvement.

Standard visual explanation metrics (pointing game, insertion/deletion) measure proxy properties, not faithfulness to the model's actual decision process; a slot attending to an incorrect region for the right reason can pass both.

Confusion matrix metric improvements are threshold-sensitive and class-imbalance-sensitive; the claimed advantage may reflect implicit threshold adjustment rather than structural classification improvement.
